parties.name: Social Democrats & Greens
parties.description: Focus on climate, welfare, and public services. Typical themes: green transition, healthcare, education.

parties.name: Liberals & Centrists
parties.description: Market-friendly with social safety nets. Often stress economy, EU integration, and civil rights.

parties.name: Conservatives & Right
parties.description: Emphasis on tradition, law and order, lower taxes, and national identity. Often stress security and borders.

skills.title: Critical thinking
skills.description: Weigh policies, spot misinformation, and form your own views from evidence.

skills.title: Civic literacy
skills.description: Understand how elections, parliaments, and governments actually work.

skills.title: Informed debate
skills.description: Discuss issues clearly and respectfully with people who disagree.

skills.title: Accountability
skills.description: Hold representatives to their promises and use your vote to demand change.

quizzes.question: What is the main purpose of voting in a democracy?
options: To choose representatives and influence policy

quizzes.question: Which of these is usually a responsibility of citizens in democratic elections?
options: Registering to vote and showing up on election day
Running the counting of ballots personally

quizzes.question: Why does voter turnout matter?
options: Elections better reflect the will of the people when more people vote

quizzes.question: What can you do if you cannot vote in person on election day?
options: Use postal or early voting if available in your country
Send a friend to vote for you
Nothing; you must vote in person

quizzes.question: Getting informed before voting typically includes:
options: Reading party programmes and checking fact-based sources
Copying the vote of a celebrity
